name: Aday (Adrian Richardson)
teaser: Chiptune, breakcore, and live visuals from Melbourne. Clan Analogue artist; MacroVerse Fringe alum. Trackers, VJ nights, and browser shader rigs.
imageCredit: Clan Analogue artist profile // 8bit domination AU geoshed
clanBio: Old-school machines (Amiga 1200, Atari 1040 STE, LSDJ Gameboy, MidiNES) meet trackers, grooveboxes, and VSTs. The hack/DIY side of making music matters: MIDI, OSC, and getting gear to talk. I VJ at breakcore and chiptune nights, build audio-reactive installs, and ran WeeklyBeats from 2012-2018. Releases on Clan Analogue: CA055M (Ming One | Addiction), CA053 (Mobile Strategies).
macroverseBio: MacroVerse began as a live sonic and visual work with Reductionist (Nick Wilson): universe evolution from energy stasis toward heat-death, at Abbotsford Convent during Melbourne Fringe. Reductionist performed on battery-powered micro-instruments; I projected live video that bent time and scale. Longtime Clan Analogue members. macroverse.aday.net.au carries that practice forward as a browser shader/VJ instrument.
